---
title: "Dataset"
---

## Overview

This project analyzes French electoral manifestos using a processed dataset built from the **Archelec archive**.

The objective is to study how manifesto themes vary across political parties, socio-professional characteristics, and election years.

## Data source

The original manifesto material comes from the **Archelec archive** developed by Sciences Po / CEVIPOF.

In this MLOps version of the project, the training pipeline does not read raw files directly from the repository. Instead, it loads a merged dataset from external object storage.

## Pipeline input

The training pipeline reads a Parquet file provided through the `URL_RAW` environment variable.

This design keeps the repository lightweight while making the project reproducible on another machine.

## What the dataset contains

The working dataset combines:

- manifesto text
- election year information
- party-related metadata
- socio-professional metadata
- cleaned text fields used in preprocessing and modeling

These fields allow the project to connect textual themes with political and candidate characteristics.

## Why Parquet is used

The processed dataset is stored in **Parquet** format because it is efficient for tabular data workflows and convenient for reproducible machine learning pipelines.

It is also easier to reuse in training than a collection of heterogeneous raw source files.

## What is not versioned in Git

To keep the repository clean and portable, the following elements are intentionally not stored in version control:

- raw data files
- processed outputs
- trained model artifacts
- logs
- secrets and local environment files

## Reproducibility note

The repository includes the code, configuration, and environment definition needed to rerun the pipeline, while the dataset itself is accessed externally through a declared configuration variable.

This separation is important for reproducibility, deployment, and collaboration.
